description: Set up Supabase client integration: install @supabase/supabase-js, create client utility, add basic database types, and prepare the project for backend connectivity.
steps: 2. Create lib/supabase.ts (or app/lib/supabase.ts depending on Next.js convention used): Export a createClient function that reads NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from environment variables. Include both a browser client and a server-side client helper.
3. Create lib/types/database.ts: Define TypeScript types for the core database tables (listings, applications, profiles) matching the schema from prd.md.
4. Create a .env.local.example file documenting required environment variables: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY.
5. Update next.config.ts if needed to allow Supabase domains in images or other config.
acceptance_criteria: lib/supabase.ts exports a working createClient function
lib/types/database.ts defines types for listings, applications, profiles tables
npm run build passes (Supabase client should not break build even without env vars - use conditional checks)
verification: Run `npm run build` to confirm build still passes
Run `npm run lint` to confirm no lint errors
